# REGIME READ — 2026-07-24

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 4 → 4 (held) — but the sub-thesis INVERTED.** Last read was Stage 4 *stabilizing* (200DMA reclaimed, one of four Stage-3 conditions fired). This read is Stage 4 **re-escalating** — the stabilization broke in a single session. VIX (18.90) still below 20 and VXN (28.06) still below 30 are the only two facts holding this out of Stage 5.
- **THE DOMINANT FACT: the 200DMA reclaim — last read's celebrated milestone — REVERSED in one day.** TQQQ closed **$66.89, roughly −4.8% to −5.9%** on Jul 23 (worst-case 3× read of NDX −1.87%), from $70.28. That puts it **−1.8% BELOW the 200DMA ($68.09)** — the shelf held for exactly one read. **The prior read's single most important bearish trigger FIRED.**
- **The worst tech session of the sequence.** **Tesla −15% (Q2 earnings miss + opex outrunning revenue); Alphabet −7% (2026 capex raised to $195–205B from $180–190B — AI-capex-without-return fear).** Nasdaq Comp **−2.15%**, NDX **−1.87%**, SPX −1.21%, DJI −0.97%, Russell −0.67%. The Big-Tech-earnings gap risk the prior read flagged **fired to the downside.**
- **Vol RE-EXPANDED — the exact asymmetry the prior Event Playbook warned of.** VXN **26.74 → 28.06 (+4.94%)** — the stall didn't just fail to resume its decline, it reversed UP toward 30. VIX **18.25 → 18.90 (+1.07%)**, now 1.1 from its bearish line. Last read: *"the asymmetry runs toward vol re-expansion rather than further crush."* **It ran that way.**
- **War escalation is now a live market driver, not a background risk.** **US 12th consecutive night of strikes on Iran; Houthi cruise/ballistic/drone attack on two Saudi tankers (Encelia, Layla) in the Red Sea; Trump threatening to destroy an Iranian bridge/power plant per Strait-of-Hormuz ship attack.** **Brent briefly topped $100**, then round-tripped to **91.96 (−8.67% on Jul 24)** — violent two-way vol on a shooting war over ~20% of world oil transit.
- **Total score: −2 → −5.** Dealer **−1 → −2** (vol re-expanding into FOMC). Breadth **0 → −1** (worst tech day, all-red, 52W gaps widened materially). Geopolitical **0 → −1** (active war escalation, oil to $100). Macro held −1 (10Y up a **fourth** session). Positioning/Cross-asset/Sentiment held 0.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bearish — TQQQ loses 200DMA below ~$68:** **✅ FIRED.** $66.89, −1.8% below.
  - **Bearish — weak Big Tech guidance re-widens drawdowns:** **✅ FIRED.** Tesla −15%, Alphabet −7% drove NDX −1.87%.
  - **Bearish — VIX through 20 and holds:** **NOT fired — NEAREST, 18.90 and climbing** (1.1 away).
  - **Bearish — VXN reverses above 30:** **NOT fired — but re-expanding hard** (28.06, +4.94%, 1.94 away).
  - **Bearish — drawdowns re-widen (SPX −5%, DJI −4%, Russell −6%):** **PARTIAL** — widened (SPX −2.8%, DJI −3.0%, Russell −3.5% vs 52W high) but not to thresholds.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **STILL UNMEASURED. Week 16.**
  - **Bullish — VXN below 25:** **REVERSED — moved the wrong way** to 28.06.
  - **Bullish — VIX below 16:** **NOT fired** (18.90).
  - **Bullish — second advancing NDX:** **NOT fired** (NDX −1.87%).
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer −1→−2, Breadth 0→−1, Geopolitical 0→−1.

> **Snapshot mix (~1-session spread):** equities + **VXN** + 10Y = **Jul 23 close**; VIX + DXY + Brent + Gold = **Jul 24 intraday.** Same offset as prior reads. This read it cuts the *other* way from last time: **VXN is the Jul-23 close showing the +4.94% re-expansion; VIX is the fresher Jul-24 print still only +1.07%** — so the vol complex has firmed on *both* gauges, and the Jul-24 tape had not yet fully re-rated when VIX printed.
>
> **Headline #1 — the 200DMA reclaim reversed in one session.** TQQQ $66.89, −1.8% below the $68.09 line it reclaimed last read. The one Stage-3 condition that had fired is un-fired.
> **Headline #2 — worst tech session of the run.** Tesla −15%, Alphabet −7%, Nasdaq −2.15% on AI-capex fear.
> **Headline #3 — vol re-expanded, not crushed.** VXN +4.94% to 28.06 into FOMC — the prior read's named asymmetry played out.
> **Headline #4 — a live war is now the tape's second driver.** 12th night of US strikes on Iran + Houthi Red Sea tanker attack; Brent topped $100 then round-tripped to $92.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 4 — Regime-change (risk-off), RE-ESCALATING.** Held out of Stage 5 only by VIX < 20 (18.90) and VXN < 30 (28.06). The stabilization thesis is dead: 200DMA lost, vol re-expanding, breadth deteriorating, worst tech session of the episode.
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 16, 2026 — VXN re-spike + semis-led all-red tape. **Unchanged; session 7. The Jul-21 relief bounce is now fully retraced.**
- **Position:** Stage 4, ~7 sessions in, **re-deteriorating.** **Stage-4 grind/re-escalation 60% (up from 47%):** the marginal favorite, and now clearly bearish-leaning. **Stage-5 escalation 30% (up from 8%):** VIX 1.1 from its line, VXN 1.94 from its line, an active war, and a hawkish-hike-risk FOMC in 3 sessions. **Stage-3 re-stabilization 10% (down from 45%):** the reclaim failed.
- **Confidence: Medium (held).** The structural blind spot (TQQQ price) stayed closed — $66.89 is a live read — and the direction is now unambiguous. Offsetting the clarity: HY OAS still unmeasured (Week 16), and the FOMC + remaining Mag-7 earnings can reprice everything Jul 27–29. Net Medium.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3):** TQQQ **reclaims and holds the 200DMA (~$68)** AND **VXN back below 25** AND **a clean, non-hawkish FOMC (hold, dovish tone)** that crushes vol rather than re-expanding it.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX through 20 and holds** (18.90, +1.07% — nearest) OR **VXN above 30** (28.06, +4.94% — closing fast) OR **a hawkish-surprise / rate-HIKE FOMC** (~1-in-3 priced) OR **drawdowns widen to SPX −5% / DJI −4% / Russell −6%** OR **a Strait-of-Hormuz closure / direct tanker-war escalation** OR HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured, Week 16).

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **holds Stage 4 but has re-escalated from stabilizing to breaking-down, with Medium confidence.** In one session the prior read's centerpiece — the 200DMA reclaim — **reversed** (TQQQ $66.89, −1.8% below $68.09); Big Tech earnings gapped down (**Tesla −15%, Alphabet −7%**) for the **worst tech session of the episode** (Nasdaq −2.15%); **VXN re-expanded +4.94% to 28.06** exactly as the prior Event Playbook warned; and a **live war escalation** (12th night of US strikes on Iran, Houthi Red Sea tanker attack, Brent briefly $100) became the tape's second driver. Total falls **−2 → −5** (Dealer −2, Macro −1, Breadth −1, Geopolitical −1). Only VIX < 20 and VXN < 30 keep this out of Stage 5 — and both lines are within ~2 points, with an FOMC carrying a ~1-in-3 hike risk landing in three sessions.

## Diverging Signals
- **The clearest single-session reversal of the sequence.** Everything the prior read credited as constructive un-wound at once: 200DMA reclaimed → **lost**; VXN draining → **re-expanding**; relief bounce → **fully retraced.** The prior read's caution ("paused into a stacked wall of event risk") was vindicated; the wall fell on the market.
- **Two engines pushing the same way for once.** For three reads the vol complex and the tape disagreed on magnitude. This session **earnings (Tesla/Alphabet) and geopolitics (Iran/Red Sea) both pushed risk-off simultaneously** — a rare alignment, and why the score moved 3 points.
- **Brent round-tripped AGAIN — the volatility is the signal.** Prior read: collapse → re-ignition to $95. This read: spike to **$100 → −8.67% to $92.** A commodity swinging ±8–10% on war headlines is itself a risk-off internal, independent of the level.
- **Macro is the quiet, persistent drag it has been all along.** **10Y up a fourth straight session** into an FOMC with live hike risk. A market breaking down against rising yields and a hawkish-reputation chair has no rate tailwind.
- **The strongest-and-weakest-in-one-instrument tension resolved bearish.** Last read TQQQ was both the best signal (200DMA reclaim) and the most ambiguous (RSI). This read the ambiguity resolved down — the reclaim failed and the instrument led the market lower at 3× beta.
- **Still blind on credit — Week 16, now into an FOMC + a war.** HY OAS remains the missing gauge for whether this is a contained repricing or the start of a spread-widening leg.

Confirms the 200DMA loss on real data. RSI directional but imprecise; IV/Greeks/put-call not reliably searchable — marked estimate/unavailable per hard rules.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum: bearish, with the mean-reversion tailwind partly re-arming.** Two down days (−1.53%, then ~−5.6%) knocked RSI back toward the low-to-mid 40s — off overbought, toward oversold, but not extended. The oversold-stabilizing premium tailwind that made mid-July distinctive is not present here: this is oversold-and-still-falling, not oversold-and-turning.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS (barely — leaning toward the DOWNTURN edge).** Recent 4-week window ≈ $57 low to ~$76 high: $66.89 is **~−12% below the ~$76 4-wk high** (NOT past −20% → not DOWNTURN, even with VXN ≥ 25) and **~+17% above the ~$57 4-wk low** (not RALLY). **→ SIDEWAYS**, but a further ~−9% (or a fresh lower 4-wk high) flips it to DOWNTURN. Flagged as still SIDEWAYS, weakening.
- **Trend: below BOTH moving averages — the 200DMA flipped from reclaimed support to overhead resistance.** ~−12.4% under the 50DMA ($76.39) and now **−1.8% under the 200DMA ($68.09)**. Whether TQQQ can reclaim $68 through the FOMC is the pivotal technical question, and it is now fighting *up* to it, not defending *down* from it.
- **Volatility & premium environment: rich AND re-expanding — the drain reversed.** VXN **28.06, +4.94%**; est. TQQQ IV **~84%**; EM **±11.9%.** The characterization flips a fourth time: "expanding → draining → drain paused → **re-expanding into the events.**" Premium is fat, but it is fat *because* the underlying is breaking down into a war and a Fed decision — swing risk is elevated, not benign.
- **Put-selling context: HOSTILE (downgraded from CAUTIOUS).** Against the backdrop: (a) **the 200DMA reclaim reversed** — TQQQ is back below both MAs; (b) **vol re-expanded** (VXN +4.94%, VIX toward 20) rather than crushing; (c) **worst tech session of the run** on Tesla/Alphabet, with more Mag-7 to report Jul 27–31; (d) an **FOMC in 3 sessions carrying ~1-in-3 hike odds** under a hawkish-reputation chair; (e) an **active, escalating war** with oil round-tripping $100; (f) 10Y up a fourth session; (g) HY OAS unmeasured (Week 16). Rich premium does not offset a breakdown into a stacked FOMC + earnings + war window — assignment and gap risk both rose. **Net: HOSTILE.** Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3 / stabilization):** **TQQQ reclaims and holds the 200DMA (~$68)** AND **VXN back below 25** AND **a clean, non-hawkish FOMC (hold + dovish tone)** that crushes rather than re-expands vol.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX through 20 and holds** (18.90 — nearest) OR **VXN above 30** (28.06, +4.94% — closing fast) OR **a hawkish-surprise / rate-HIKE FOMC** (~1-in-3 priced) OR **drawdowns widen to SPX −5% / DJI −4% / Russell −6%** OR **a Strait-of-Hormuz closure or direct tanker-war escalation** OR HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured, Week 16). **TQQQ losing the 13-wk low (~$57)** would confirm a fresh leg.
- **Neutralizer:** **the FOMC Jul 28–29 (decision + presser Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET, chair Warsh, no SEP)** and the **remaining Mag-7 earnings (Jul 27–31)** — either can reprice the read in one session. Vol is re-expanding *into* both, and a war is running underneath — the asymmetry is toward further re-expansion.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jul 22 (after close, realized Jul 23):** **Alphabet −7%** (capex to $195–205B) & **Tesla −15%** (Q2 miss) — drove the Jul-23 breakdown ([Motley Fool Jul 23](https://www.fool.com/coverage/stock-market-today/2026/07/23/stock-market-today-july-23-tesla-drops-15-leading-tech-stock-slide/), [TheStreet Jul 23](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-july-23-2026)).
- **Jul 27–31, 2026:** **Remaining Mag-7 earnings cluster** — peak single-name concentration risk for NDX/TQQQ.
- **Jul 28–29, 2026:** **FOMC decision** (statement + presser Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET; chair Warsh; no SEP; hold at 3.50–3.75% base case, **~1-in-3 hike priced**) — **the critical scheduled event, inside the window** ([Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/event/fed-decision-in-july-181), [Forbes Jul 23](https://www.forbes.com/sites/simonmoore/2026/07/23/markets-see-chance-fed-hikes-next-week-at-july-meeting/), [Fed calendar](https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm)).
- **Jul 30, 2026:** Q2 GDP Advance, 8:30 am ET — just outside the window.
- **Ongoing, unscheduled:** the Iran/Red Sea war (12th night of US strikes; Houthi tanker attacks; Strait-of-Hormuz risk); whether Brent re-tests $100; whether VIX takes 20 / VXN takes 30.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- **Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jul 24, 27, 28, 29, 30):** **FOMC Jul 28–29** (presser Jul 29), the **bulk of the Mag-7 earnings cluster (Jul 27–31)**, and the **live Iran/Red Sea war** as an ongoing unscheduled shock.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — a scheduled central-bank decision **and** mega-cap earnings **and** an active geopolitical conflict inside the next 5 sessions. Event, single-name, and geopolitical risk are **stacked three deep.**

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Last **Jun 19, 2026** (digested). Next **Sep 18, 2026** — **~38 trading days out. OUTSIDE the 2-session window.**
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Last **Jun 30, 2026** (digested). Next **Sep 30, 2026** — **~46 trading days out. OUTSIDE the 2-session window.**
- **Net:** No structural calendar items inside the 2-session window. Intraday volume/pinning is **not** structurally elevated by calendar mechanics. The −2.15% breakdown, the VXN re-expansion, the 200DMA loss, and the oil round-trip are genuine directional/event/war signals — not expiry or rebalancing artifacts. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook

- **FOMC, Jul 28–29, 2026 (decision + presser Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET; no SEP; chair Kevin Warsh; ~1-in-3 hike priced).** Retrievable named history for the closest setup — an FOMC arriving with vol *re-expanding* off a tech-led breakdown, inside an unresolved risk-off episode, with a live geopolitical war and non-trivial hike odds — is limited, and I will not manufacture averages. Bracketing episodes: **FOMC Sep 21, 2022** — SPX −1.7% on the day and lower for sessions after, VIX *rose through and past* the meeting rather than crushing (the analogue for vol that is already firming into the decision). **FOMC Dec 14, 2022** — SPX ~−2.5% as a hawkish message overrode a soft prior CPI, showing a pre-meeting bounce offers no protection through a hawkish surprise (directly relevant with ~1-in-3 hike odds live). **FOMC Aug 2024 (post-yen-carry)** — the counter-case: a vol spike drained into the meeting, the Fed didn't re-spike it, and the episode ended. **The current setup sits closer to the 2022 family than to Aug-2024**, because vol is *re-expanding into* this decision, not draining.
- **On vol behavior:** the crush-into-a-decision pattern fails precisely when the Fed surprises hawkish (the 2022 cases) — and this meeting carries live hike odds. Current detail: **VXN already re-expanded +4.94% to 28.06 and VIX is grinding toward 20** — pre-event vol that is *rising* into the decision has little crush to deliver and ample room to spike on a hawkish print or a hike, an asymmetry sharpened by new-chair message uncertainty and the overlapping Mag-7 earnings.
- **Net alert:** the record is two-sided but the current configuration tilts toward the **2022 hawkish-repricing family**: vol is re-expanding into the meeting, hike odds are ~1-in-3, a new hawkish-reputation chair adds message risk, Mag-7 earnings stack index and single-name risk in the same week, **and a live war is repricing oil in ±8–10% daily swings.** **Gap risk through Jul 27–29 is materially elevated and the asymmetry runs toward further vol re-expansion, not crush.** Descriptive only — no trade calls.

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - **Analysis only.** No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - **All index/vol/yield/commodity levels used VERBATIM from the supplied LIVE MARKET SNAPSHOT.** No index levels web-searched.
> - **Snapshot timing spread disclosed:** equities + VXN + 10Y = Jul 23 close; VIX + DXY + Brent + Gold = Jul 24 intraday. Noted in the snapshot block, Dealer lens, and Diverging Signals.
> - **Brent reconciliation:** the snapshot's Brent −8.67% (Jul 24) is a *round-trip DOWN* from the ~$100 war spike on Jul 23 (Houthi tanker attack) — both facts stated; the war premium and the two-way vol are treated as the signal, scored in Geopolitical (−1) to avoid double-counting in Cross-asset (held 0).
